channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Gameplay Trailer Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Just a few days ahead of release, Fortnite has provided players with a glimpse of the gameplay they can expect in Chapter 7 Season 3.

Chapter 7 Season 3 doesn’t start until June 6, 2026, and that will also come after the live event for Season 2, which takes place on the 5th. The brand new season comes with a wealth of new content that will help players ‘Power Up’, build a collection, and claim victory. Check out everything new in the trailer below:

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Come with Creatures that Pack A Punch

The new season, titled “Runners”, features several small creatures that seemingly provide players with extra power. The Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3’s gameplay trailer specifically showcased two instances where the players’ weapons power up just by having one of these creatures on their backs. A shotgun was able to blow up a car in one shot, whereas an assault rifle was easily able to take down metal buildings. There seem to be some traversal power-ups, too, letting the character jump higher and seemingly run and slide faster.

The cover image for Fortnite’s new season gives players a sense of the types of skins they can expect. There seems to be a pen and ink version of John Wick, along with a dog with the same art style that players can grab. There’s also a collaboration with BurntPeanut, who appears as one of the small creatures/sidekicks/sprites that players have seen in the new trailer, as well as the cover image.

In other news, Fortnite players can watch the first 10 minutes of the recently released The Mandalorian and Grogu movie right now. Additionally, here are all the details on the free rewards available during Fortnite Community Day, along with an overview of the headline additions to the Item Shop. What are your thoughts on Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3’s gameplay trailer? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Delta Force preparing for a UGC initiative that can pay players. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Team WE Descend LPL Playoffs Descend Into Chaos
item.content:encoded:
The LPL Split 2 Playoffs have not shaken out how anyone expected, as Team WE have recorded multiple shock victories to reach the Upper Bracket final.

Meanwhile, JD Gaming (JDG), Anyone’s Legends, and reigning champions Bilibili Gaming (BLG) are all in the Lower Bracket.

LPL and First Stand Champions BLG were heavy favourites for the Playoffs. They finished the regular season top after losing just two series, and all five BLG players made the LPL All-Pro team.

Meanwhile, Team WE finished bottom of the LPL’s top eight seeds in Group Ascend and barely made it into the Playoffs after beating LNG Esports 3-2 in the Play-In.

Yet, the team that won only one of their 14 regular-season series beat First Stand champions BLG 3-1 and swept Esports World Cup-qualified Anyone’s Legend 3-0.

LPL Split 2 Playoffs Bracket

Joining Team WE in the Upper Bracket final is Top Esports (TES), as one of the two are guaranteed to qualify for MSI.

And both LPL seeds will skip the Play-In thanks to BLG’s First Stand triumph.

Top Esports have improved a lot since the start of the season, with former world champions Gao ‘Tian’ Tian-Liang and Yu ‘JackeyLove’ Wen-Bo returning to the line-up.

After sweeping LNG Esports, TES defeated a strong JDG roster 3-1 to book their spot in the Upper Bracket.

Meanwhile, the Lower Bracket is a nightmare, stacked with ridiculous teams in elimination matches.

Anyone’s Legends will try to bounce back against the winners of ThunderTalk Gaming and LDG Gaming.

Meanwhile, BLG will have to defeat EDward Gaming, JD Gaming, and then potentially Anyone’s Legend and Team WE or TES to keep the dream of a Golden Road, winning every title available in a season, alive.

Who do you expect will win the LPL Playoffs and proceed to MSI? Share your predictions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Elsewhere, T1 have extended CEO Joe Marsh’s contract until 2029.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Delta Force Preparing For UGC Initiative That Could Pay Players
item.content:encoded:
It has been discovered that Tencent is preparing some kind of UGC (User-Generated Content) initiative for Delta Force, the cross-platform first-person shooter that has amassed tens of millions of players. The title has been on the market for more than a year, competing with titles like Battlefield and Escape from Tarkov and offering an array of game modes.

This UGC push was unveiled following the discovery of a job listing that talked about hiring a ‘User-Generated Content Designer’ working specifically on Delta Force. The team wants someone with three or more years of UGC platform product experience and will help lead the UGC business direction.

Delta Force Players Could Earn Soon

Fortnite really pushed the popularization of UGC ecosystems to the forefront of gaming in recent years. With the UEFN, some users have been able to create masterful experiences that generated a surprising amount of income. It’s the same concept as Roblox, which allows users to earn vast sums of real-world cash for their in-game creations.

Delta Force could be the next game to push into the UGC space, a revelation that comes courtesy of a Tencent job listing for a UGC Designer working on the first-person shooter. This information came from Game Daily via PocketGamer.biz.

UGC is a relatively easy way for a developer to push their game to the limit. It encourages fans to bring to life creations in games made using the same tools that the developers use to craft base experiences. It’s a similar concept to modding, but typically, UGC is an on-platform, officially supported model using the best tools available.

It’s done in a way that encourages users to learn from the ground up and offers them an easier way into the modding space, because more content is never a bad thing.

Delta Force is one of the most popular shooters in the world, easily outpacing some of the AAA titles that fill the space. At the time of writing, it boasted a whopping 103,032 players on Steam, beating the likes of Battlefield 6, Call of Duty, Overwatch, ARC Raiders, Escape from Tarkov, and many more.

Would you participate in a Delta Force UGC ecosystem? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the report that a Baldur’s Gate 2 remake is in development

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Project Motor Racing Dev Says Team “Let Players Down” After Troubled Launch
item.content:encoded:
Few racing games have faced a launch as difficult as Project Motor Racing.

When it launched, players encountered bugs, stability issues, online connectivity problems, and a host of technical concerns that quickly led to negative reviews and widespread criticism from the sim racing community. Months later, the game is still working to recover from that first impression.

For Aris Vasilakos, who joined the Project Motor Racing team at Straight4 Studios in May after helping build acclaimed racing simulators including Assetto Corsa and Assetto Corsa Competizione, the challenge has been understanding both where the game fell short and what it needs to become moving forward.

Speaking with Insider Gaming at the SimRacing Expo in Charlotte, Vasilakos acknowledged the impact the troubled launch had on both players and Straight4 Studios.

Morale Took A Hit Following Launch

“Morale was really at the minimum levels,” Vasilakos said. “Developers for video games are like artists. They all pretend that they don’t really care that much about the feedback, but they do. You feel it a lot because your job gets out there for people to enjoy and people do not enjoy it and get back to you and complain.”

The situation became especially difficult because developers expected the game’s release to mark the end of an intense development cycle. Instead, it immediately became the beginning of another.

  • More Sim Racing: The Best Things I Saw At SimRacing Expo Charlotte 2026

“Our guys have been working like crazy before the release,” he said. “When they were kind of ready that, alright, finally we’re releasing and now we’re going to have fun, they went into a situation that they had to work even harder to first fix the game and then improve the game.”

While Vasilakos was not part of the team when Project Motor Racing launched, he said he quickly understood why players reacted the way they did.

The game’s early reviews reflected widespread frustration, and the team was forced to evaluate not only what went wrong technically but also what modern racing fans expect from a simulation title.

Still, despite the criticism, one thing never changed.

“We still want to make a hardcore simulation, and this is where we should be going,” Vasilakos said. “That hasn’t changed.”

Fixing The Foundation

According to Vasilakos, the team’s first priority was stabilizing the game. Crashes, performance issues, and inconsistent experiences across different hardware configurations all needed to be addressed before anything else.

“Number one, the game has to be stable,” he said. “If you have your game crashing, even if you have the best possible experience, nobody is going to play.”

Performance improvements followed, particularly for players using lower-end systems. Since launch, Straight4 Studios has released multiple updates designed to improve stability, boost performance, and create a more consistent experience across PC and console platforms.

Those efforts are beginning to show results.

Vasilakos pointed to the game’s improving Steam reviews as one sign that perceptions are starting to change.

“We managed to take the game from a very low Steam rating,” he said. “Right now, it’s 60%. So there has been a massive improvement on the recent reviews.”

He also noted that more players are beginning to revisit the game after initially writing it off.

“A lot of players start looking at us right now,” he said. “They tried here, and they get up and they’re like, ‘that’s much better than I thought.'”

The Work Is Far From Finished

That does not mean the team believes the game is where it needs to be.

When asked what still needs the most work, Vasilakos immediately pointed to the online experience. Features that make it easier for players to join sessions, improved networking functionality, and overall accessibility remain major priorities.

The team is also focused on improving the game’s AI systems and refining smaller details throughout the experience.

“We need to improve the online experience,” he said. “Then we need to improve the AI.”

Even seemingly minor issues are being examined. Vasilakos pointed to several areas where the underlying simulation systems are more advanced than the game’s interface communicates, creating the impression that features are incomplete even when the technology behind them is functioning correctly.

That attention to detail, he believes, will ultimately help rebuild trust with players.

For now, the team finds itself in the unusual position of being viewed as an underdog.

“We are kind of the underdog that everybody expects to underdeliver,” Vasilakos said. “But we’re actually overdelivering from what they expect.”

Whether that momentum continues will depend on the team’s ability to keep improving the game while simultaneously delivering the content updates and DLC promised to players.

As for those who bought Project Motor Racing at launch, experienced its problems, and moved on, Vasilakos is not interested in asking for forgiveness.

Instead, he believes the team must earn it.

“I cannot convince them and I respect them, and I’m very sorry about it,” he said. “The only thing we can do is work and make it worthy. If it is worthy, they will come back.”

In other news, read about PlayStation eliminating more than 1,000 shovelware games from its store. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
